title: Unauthorized Change Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: NERC CIP-010-4 R1 Configuration change management — detecting changes outside the approved baseline is the direct control for R1.4 monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of NERC CIP CIP-010-4 R1 (Configuration change management) — Splunk UC-16.4.1: Unauthorized Change Detection.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-16.4.1' running on sourcetype=snow:change_request, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of IT-Grundschutz OPS.1.1.2 (Ordered ICT operation) — Splunk UC-16.4.1: Unauthorized Change Detection.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-16.4.1' running on sourcetype=snow:change_request, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of MAS TRM §4.1.1 (Technology risk governance) — Splunk UC-16.4.1: Unauthorized Change Detection.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX ITGC change-authorization control — unauthorised change detection is the canonical program-change ITGC tested by SOX auditors to confirm changes were authorised before promotion to production.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of SOX-ITGC ITGC.ChangeMgmt.Authorization (Change authorised) — Splunk UC-16.4.1: Unauthorized Change Detection.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-16.4.1' running on sourcetype=snow:change_request, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request"
| eval approved=coalesce(approval,"") 
| where match(lower(u_authorization),"(?i)unauthorized|rejected") OR (isnull(cab_date) AND lower(type)!="standard" AND lower(category)!="routine")
| table _time, number, short_description, state, type, u_authorization, opened_by
| sort -_time
description: Changes executed without approval or outside policy create audit exposure and outage risk; detecting them early supports SOC-2/ITIL controls and rapid rollback decisions.
value: Changes executed without approval or outside policy create audit exposure and outage risk; detecting them early supports SOC-2/ITIL controls and rapid rollback decisions.
implementation: Ingest change_request with approval, CAB, and authorization fields mapped from ServiceNow. Build allowlists for standard/pre-approved change models. Alert when production-impacting changes lack `cab_date` or show `rejected`/`unauthorized` authorization. Correlate with CMDB and deployment tools for out-of-band activity.
visualization: Table (flagged changes), Single value (unauthorized count — target: 0), Timeline (violations).
grandmaExplanation: Changes executed without approval or outside policy create audit exposure and outage risk; detecting them early supports SOC-2/ITIL controls and rapid rollback decisions — so tickets, changes, and incidents are on track and nothing is silently missed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest change_request with approval, CAB, and authorization fields mapped from ServiceNow. Build allowlists for standard/pre-approved change models. Alert when production-impacting changes lack `cab_date` or show `rejected`/`unauthorized` authorization. Correlate with CMDB and deployment tools for out-of-band activity.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request"
| eval approved=coalesce(approval,"") 
| where match(lower(u_authorization),"(?i)unauthorized|rejected") OR (isnull(cab_date) AND lower(type)!="standard" AND lower(category)!="routine")
| table _time, number, short_description, state, type, u_authorization, opened_by
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unauthorized Change Detection** — Changes executed without approval or outside policy create audit exposure and outage risk; detecting them early supports SOC-2/ITIL controls and rapid rollback decisions.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=snow:change_request`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:change_request". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **approved** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(u_authorization),"(?i)unauthorized|rejected") OR (isnull(cab_date) AND lower(type)!="standard" AND lower(…` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Unauthorized Change Detection**): table _time, number, short_description, state, type, u_authorization, opened_by
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (flagged changes), Single value (unauthorized count — target: 0), Timeline (violations).
